generated_from: expo/docs/pages/build @ 2026-06-06; hardened 2026-06-07 (routes /github + /builds confirmed from universe SidebarNavigation + next.config.ts; themes confirmed dark from the doc images; Build triggers confirmed being sunset per a universe TODO). All 19 build-section screenshots live in building-from-github.mdx; the other 9 build pages have none (orbit.mdx is an .mp4).
state_warning: These are setup-FLOW shots and each needs a DIFFERENT project state (no-GitHub-link -> linked-no-repo -> repo-connected -> triggers-configured), so NO single project renders all of them at once. Set `project` (or pass --project=<slug>) to a project with the EAS GitHub app installed and a repo connected. Even then expect a subset to ERROR/unsure because their state does not match. A fresh non-connected project only renders project-github-page (the empty 'Link your GitHub user' state). Build triggers shots additionally require the (deprecated, being-sunset) triggers feature, which may be absent on projects created after the cutoff.

screenshots.depicts: The project GitHub settings page with the empty 'Link your GitHub user to Expo to continue' state (Link your GitHub repository to your Expo project)
screenshots.scope_note: Project Configuration > GitHub settings page on the EAS dashboard. [account]/[project] resolved by the runner. The depicted empty state only appears before a GitHub user is linked; on a linked project this page shows the connected-repository UI instead.

screenshots.depicts: The GitHub 'Install & Authorize Expo' permissions screen (Install the Expo GitHub App)
screenshots.scope_note: Out of scope: this is a github.com app-installation page (browser window on github.com/apps/expo), not an Expo/EAS dashboard surface. Not a stable dashboard screen the screenshot lane can compare against.

screenshots.depicts: The 'Connect a GitHub repository' panel with the repository list and Connect buttons on the project GitHub settings page (Link the GitHub repository to your Expo project)
screenshots.scope_note: Connect-a-repository panel on the project GitHub settings page (dashboard). Only shown once the GitHub user is linked and before a repo is connected; capture is read-only, NEVER click Connect.

screenshots.depicts: The GitHub account selector dropdown showing the 'Add new account' option on the project GitHub settings page (Add a repository from a different GitHub account)
reach: on the Connect a GitHub repository panel, open the GitHub account selector dropdown to reveal the Add new account option
screenshots.scope_note: Cropped view of the account-selector dropdown on the project GitHub settings page (dashboard). Opening the dropdown is read-only; do not select Add new account. Requires the connect-a-repository panel to be present (linked GitHub user, repo not yet connected).

screenshots.depicts: The Base directory input with a Save button on the project GitHub settings page (Configure your repository settings)
screenshots.scope_note: Base directory card on the project GitHub settings page (dashboard). Only present once a repository is connected. Capture read-only, NEVER click Save; scroll_to brings the card into view.

screenshots.depicts: The 'Start a build from GitHub' modal with repository, base directory, platform, git ref, and build profile fields (Build using the Expo website)
reach: on the project builds list page, click the 'Build from GitHub' button to open the Start a build from GitHub modal
screenshots.scope_note: Modal reached from the project builds list page (dashboard). Opening the modal is read-only; NEVER click Confirm, which would start a build. Requires a connected GitHub repository for the button to appear.

screenshots.depicts: A GitHub PR with the eas-build label applied and live EAS Build status checks (Build using GitHub PR labels)
screenshots.scope_note: Out of scope: this is the GitHub.com pull request checks UI, not the Expo/EAS dashboard. Not a stable dashboard surface.

screenshots.depicts: EAS Build check details under the Checks tab on a GitHub PR (Build using GitHub PR labels)
screenshots.scope_note: Out of scope: GitHub.com PR Checks tab, not the Expo/EAS dashboard.

screenshots.depicts: The Build triggers section in its empty 'no push to branch triggers' state with the New Build Trigger button on the project GitHub settings page (Set up build triggers)
screenshots.scope_note: Build triggers section on the project GitHub settings page (dashboard). Build triggers are a deprecated feature disabled for new projects, so this section may not render on projects created after the cutoff. Empty state requires no existing triggers; scroll_to brings the section into view.

screenshots.depicts: The default state of the 'Add a new build trigger' modal (Push to branch) with execution behavior, branch pattern, and platform fields (Set up build triggers)
reach: scroll to the Build triggers section and click New Build Trigger to open the Add a new build trigger modal
screenshots.scope_note: Modal on the project GitHub settings page (dashboard). Opening the modal is read-only; NEVER click Create trigger. Deprecated feature, may be unavailable on newer projects.

screenshots.depicts: A filled-out 'Add a new build trigger' modal with branch pattern 'main' and both platforms checked (Set up build triggers)
reach: open the Add a new build trigger modal from the Build triggers section; the doc shows it with fields filled in
screenshots.scope_note: Same trigger modal as empty-build-trigger-form but depicting a filled-in form. Capture the modal read-only; NEVER click Create trigger. Deprecated feature, may be unavailable on newer projects.

screenshots.depicts: The Build triggers table populated with two enabled 'main' push-to-branch triggers (iOS and Android) showing columns for Build Profile, Platform, Environment, Auto Submit, and Most Recent Result (Set up build triggers)
screenshots.scope_note: Build triggers section on the project GitHub settings page (dashboard) with existing triggers. Requires at least one configured trigger to show the populated table; deprecated feature, may be unavailable on newer projects.

screenshots.depicts: The GitHub checks popover on a branch commit showing EAS Build Android and iOS checks (Set up build triggers)
screenshots.scope_note: Out of scope: GitHub.com repository/branch checks popover, not the Expo/EAS dashboard.

screenshots.depicts: A full-browser screenshot of a GitHub commit page for a tag with the EAS Build checks popover (Set up build triggers)
screenshots.scope_note: Out of scope: full-browser GitHub.com commit page, not the Expo/EAS dashboard.

screenshots.depicts: The 'Add a new build trigger' modal with the Pull request trigger type selected, showing source and target branch pattern fields (Set up build triggers)
reach: open the Add a new build trigger modal, then select the Pull request trigger type tab
screenshots.scope_note: Trigger modal on the project GitHub settings page (dashboard) with the Pull request tab active. Switching tabs and capturing is read-only; NEVER click Create trigger. Deprecated feature, may be unavailable on newer projects.

screenshots.depicts: The GitHub checks section on a pull request showing a queued EAS Build Android check (Set up build triggers)
screenshots.scope_note: Out of scope: GitHub.com pull request checks section, not the Expo/EAS dashboard.

screenshots.depicts: The options dropdown on a build trigger row showing Build now, Disable trigger, Edit trigger, and Delete trigger (Manage build triggers)
reach: scroll to the Build triggers table and click the options (three-dot) button at the right of a trigger row to open its menu
screenshots.scope_note: Per-row options menu in the Build triggers table (dashboard). Opening the menu is read-only, but the menu contains destructive items (Disable, Delete) and a Build now action; capture only, do NOT click any item. Requires an existing trigger; deprecated feature.

screenshots.depicts: The EAS Submit fields on the trigger edit form: 'Automatically submit to stores after building successfully' checkbox and EAS Submit profile input (Automatic app stores submission with EAS Submit)
reach: from a trigger row options menu choose Edit trigger, then scroll to the EAS Submit section at the bottom of the edit form
screenshots.scope_note: Bottom of the Edit trigger form (dashboard modal) showing the EAS Submit fields. Opening the edit form is read-only; NEVER click Update trigger. Requires an existing trigger; deprecated feature.

screenshots.depicts: The Auto Submit column of the Build triggers table showing 'Yes' with an EAS Submit profile tooltip, alongside the Most Recent Result column (Automatic app stores submission with EAS Submit)
reach: scroll to the Build triggers table and hover the Auto Submit cell of a trigger with submission enabled to show the EAS Submit profile tooltip
screenshots.scope_note: Cropped view of the Auto Submit column in the Build triggers table (dashboard). Requires an existing trigger with automatic submission enabled; deprecated feature. Read-only.

screenshots.depicts: Demo video of Expo Orbit's basic features (Expo Orbit)
screenshots.scope_note: Out of scope: this is an .mp4 demo video of the Orbit desktop app, not a still screenshot of the Expo/EAS dashboard. Nothing for the screenshot lane to compare.

settings.scope_note: Project Configuration > GitHub settings page; the doc links here for linking a repo and configuring base directory. [account]/[project] resolved by the runner.
Base directory control documented on the project GitHub settings page. Only present once a repository is connected.
Project builds list page; the doc claims a 'Build from GitHub' button lives here. The button only appears once a GitHub repository is connected.
Build triggers section on the project GitHub settings page. Deprecated and disabled for new projects, so this control may be absent on projects created after the cutoff.
